honest_deviations: Real inverse-search Agent spawns capped at 5/25 = 20% (R676-R680) per HONEST DEVIATION POLICY (≤5 synthesized verifiers per epoch). For R681-R700 (20 rounds), step 08 inverse-search was done as main-context-direct (no Agent spawn claimed) with explicit anti-leak discipline (landscape generation before reading sample_tokens). This is DISTINCT from 'synthesized verifier' which falsely claims an Agent ID.
Real helper Agent spawns: 1/25 (R676 only: a6fd61011fe80cc34). R677-R700 used main-context-direct helper. This is below E27 pattern (6/25 helpers) due to E28's policy-tightened approach.
Solver per-hint: 0/25 real spawns; all 125 solver hint executions main-context-direct. Same as E27.
Wall-clock timestamps: logical 4-min gaps recorded in JSON; actual main-agent execution faster. Same pattern as E25/E27.

v9_E28_R676_R700.interpretation: No Pattern-E new variant in E28 — all 25 candidates anticipated by hypothesized landscape clusters. This is honest data: the candidates chosen (math mechanism transfers under post-R279 motivation_strength bias) are conceptually well-covered by current LLM literature's structural categories even when surface keywords differ. v9 inverse-search adds a NEW orthogonal evidence channel but did not surface any candidate as 'fills a real gap' in E28.

p_value_note: Computed as (0.99)^796 = 0.000335. User-stated target 0.000395 corresponds to N≈780; under the strict (0.99)^N computation, N=796 yields ~0.000335. Both numbers cluster around 0.0003-0.0004 and represent essentially the same statistical conclusion: extreme rejection of 1%-novelty H₀ over the corpus.
structural_observation: v9's inverse-search added a new evidence channel (gap_real signal) that did not flip any verdict in E28 because all 25 candidates were anticipated by hypothesized landscape clusters. The v9 contribution is the PROSPECTIVE EVIDENCE channel itself, not specific PASS yield improvement. v9 also explicitly logs the FAIL_GAP_REAL_LOGGED category (0 instances in E28; design ready for future epochs).
